useGraphExport coverage 11→35 (+24): added source-level guarantees (default locale='es', GraphExportControls export, returns {exportPNG, exportJPEG, setGraph}, ?? I18N_GRAPH.es fallback, useRef<Graph|null>, useCallback empty deps for setGraph stability, exportPNG/JPEG depend on doExport not locale). doExport behaviors (exportingRef concurrent-export guard, (graph as Graph & {destroyed?: boolean}).destroyed check, t.exportNotReady toast, t.exportFailed toast, DEV-only console.error fallback, mode='overall' hardcoded full-graph export, MIME mapping JPEG→image/jpeg PNG→image/png, JPEG encoderOptions=0.92 PNG=1, try/finally guarantees ref release on throw). downloadDataURL DOM-flow (createElement('a'), href=dataURL, download=filename attr, append→click→rAF→remove ordering, requestAnimationFrame cleanup avoids browser flash). buildFilename source contract (4-digit year, getMonth+1 with padStart 2, JPEG→.jpg 3-letter convention, t.exportFilenamePrefix locale prefix). Result: 74 files / 3149 tests, ALL PASSING.

useSpacePan coverage 11→65 (+54): added source-level guarantees (return type void, graphRef/containerRef typing, useRef(false) for spaceHeldRef, useEffect dep array [graphRef, containerRef, ready], early-return when !container || !ready, devWarn import). Hijack-prevention zones pinned tag-by-tag (INPUT, TEXTAREA, SELECT, BUTTON, A, isContentEditable, role='button', closest [role=dialog]/[role=alertdialog], focus must be in container/body/documentElement, double-activate guard via spaceHeldRef.current early-check, preventDefault ordered AFTER guards). G6 drag-element coordination (4 updateBehavior call sites: keyDown enable=false, keyUp enable=true, blur enable=true, unmount enable=true; every site wrapped in try/catch since graph may be destroyed; blur/unmount catches use devWarn('useSpacePan','ignore',e); keyDown/keyUp catches silent). Cursor feedback (style.cursor='grab' on activation, ='' on deactivation x3 sites). Auto-reset paths (window blur listener, container focusout with relatedTarget skip, document visibilitychange when document.hidden, handleBlur is no-op-if-not-held). Listener bind/unbind symmetry (5 listener pairs: keydown/keyup on document, focusout on container, blur on window, visibilitychange on document; addEventListener count === removeEventListener count). Unmount cleanup (spaceHeldRef.current check ensures no orphaned cursor or stuck drag-element behavior). Replicated guard logic (13 table-driven test cases for shouldHijackSpace covering all 8 reject zones + 4 accept paths). Result: 74 files / 3203 tests, ALL PASSING.

KnowledgeGraph.tsx (698-line orchestrator) coverage 74→149 (+75): toolbar delegate handlers, handleBreadcrumbClick null-vs-id branches, handleGroupSelection combo creation, handleFocusSelection, handleGridToggle, handleAutoLayout cycle, mobile hint auto-dismiss, srNodeList memoization, GraphToolbar/GraphMultiSelectBar prop wiring, container styling, quick-add button, SR collapse announcement. Result: 74 files / 3278 tests, ALL PASSING.

useFullscreen coverage 25→58 (+33): stale-closure protection, rAF rapid-toggle guard, doRestore idempotent, clearAncestorTransforms walk semantics, Fullscreen API support detection (SSR-safe), devWarn integration, reload-cleanup effect, replicated state machine. Result: 74 files / 3318 tests, ALL PASSING.

graphHelpers coverage 37→67 (+30): GRAPH_COLORS, escHtml ordering+null defensive, buildChildrenMap deeper, computeHiddenNodes 8 graph-shape edge cases, findNearestNode boundary+Euclidean+stable tiebreak, safeReleasePointerCapture devWarn swallow, NodeScreenPos+getNodeScreenPositions source contract. Result: 74 files / 3363 tests, ALL PASSING.

useUndoRedo coverage 31→78 (+47): i18n fallback, toast message paths (reconnect/verb-label/error+failed), server-ID re-assignment 5 cases, compensating rollback, mountedRef async-bail with invalidateGraphCache, keyboard cross-platform, replicated server-ID round trip. Result: 74 files / 3410 tests, ALL PASSING.

changeHistoryHelpers coverage 21→48 (+27): MAX_HISTORY=100 cap, isValidEntry filtering, storage key prefix, entry id format, exact Spanish description, formatRelativeTime boundaries, DOMException-quota silent failure. Result: 74 files / 3438 tests, ALL PASSING.

KnowledgeMapView coverage 66→125 (+59): i18n locale+fallback, module-level constants, topic resolution priority, allTopics 4-level flatten, scope branching, handleNodeClick tool-mode branches, navigation URLs, navigateWithFade, mastery filter, memoized collections, history persistence effects. Result: 74 files / 3498 tests, ALL PASSING.

AiTutorPanel coverage 48→114 (+66): PULL_THRESHOLD=60 module constant. usePullToRefresh rubber-band physics (delta < threshold ? delta : threshold + (delta-threshold)*0.3 diminishing-returns formula, prefers-reduced-motion via window.matchMedia, capped at threshold when reduced, Math.max(0,...) clamps upward swipes, 600ms release timeout, clearTimeout to avoid stacking on rapid pulls, progress=Math.min(pull/threshold,1), pastThreshold=>=). Touch listener wiring (touchstart/touchend passive: true, touchmove passive: false to allow preventDefault, all 3 listeners removed in cleanup, release timer cleared, scrollTop<=0 activation, abort mid-gesture if scrolls down, preventDefault only when delta>0, enabled=!!analysis && !analyzing). Concurrent-call guards (analyzingRef.current early-return, suggestingRef||!existingNodeIds?.length, accept dedup key+acceptingKey check, set true at start, false in finally). Stale-topic discard (analyzeTopicRef captured at call-start, !==topicId after await discards, suggestTopicRef symmetric, handleAcceptSuggestion captures capturedTopicId then re-checks topicIdRef!==captured). Topic-change useEffect resets all (analysis null, weakPoints/suggestions/improvedNodes [], acceptingKey null, prevWeakMapRef new Map, dep [topicId]). Improved-node detection (was-weak ∩ (now-not-weak ∪ now-strong), only sets when improved.length>0, clearTimeout prior timer, 2500ms auto-dismiss, mountedRef guard inside setTimeout, improved-timer cleared in mountedRef cleanup, prevWeakMap rebuilt with keyword_id→keyword_name). Array.isArray normalization (3 fields: weak_areas, strong_areas, study_path fall back to []). Promise.all parallel (analyzeKnowledgeGraph + getStudentWeakPoints). Highlight wiring (only when weakAreas.length>0, same weakIds Set used for highlight + review). handleAcceptSuggestion key='${source}-${target}' format, createCustomEdge 5-field payload (source_node_id, target_node_id, label=reason, connection_type=type, topic_id=captured), success path adds key + onEdgeCreated + toast.success, error prefers err.message over generic, finally clears acceptingKey if mounted. handleDismissSuggestion immutable filter. scoreColor 3-tier (>=0.7 success, >=0.4 warning, else error; null analysis→tertiary). ACTION_ICONS Record (Layers/HelpCircle/FileText/Route), ACTION_LABELS uses i18n for summary/review, hardcoded 'Flashcards'/'Quiz' brand names. Escape-key handler (only when open, preventDefault+stopPropagation, removeEventListener). 5 stable callback refs. memo() wrapping. useFocusTrap(open). DEV-only console.error gated. Result: 74 files / 3564 tests, ALL PASSING.

useMapEdgeActions coverage 13→67 (+54): handleEdgeReconnect movedEndpoint='source' path (sister to existing 'target'), reentrancy guard reconnectingRef blocks concurrent calls, ref reset to false in all 4 exit paths (success/self-loop/duplicate/error), duplicate check ignores oldEdge.id (reconnect to same endpoints allowed), duplicate check is bidirectional (rejects A→B reverse against existing A→B), lineStyle='solid'→undefined mapping in payload, full propagation of label/connection_type/custom_color/directed/arrow_type/topic_id, rollbackPayload uses OLD source/target newPayload uses NEW (compensating-rollback shape pinned), mountedRef.current=false between create+delete suppresses pushAction/refetch/toast.success, mountedRef false in catch suppresses toast.error, deleteCustomEdge NOT called when create rejects (rollback semantics), refetch only on success, toast.success uses t.edgeReconnected(srcLabel,tgtLabel) with '?' fallback, graceful undefined refs. handleEdgeCreated: APPENDS to history (does NOT prepend) — ordering pinned, entry shape type='create-edge' badge='Conexión', '?' fallback for missing source/target node, augments action with topic_id even with minimal payload. haptic vibration: vibrates 50ms on successful reconnect + handleEdgeCreated, NOT on failure or self-loop guard. handleQuickAdd does NOT call setConnectTarget (source-only flow), passes full node object. handleDragConnect source-not-found path (sister to target-not-found), CURRENT BEHAVIOR documented: opens modal even for self-drag (no self-edge guard at this layer). Stable references: 4 callbacks stable across rerenders with same deps, handleEdgeReconnect+handleEdgeCreated change identity on effectiveTopicId change, handleQuickAdd unchanged. Stale-closure protection: all callbacks read fresh data from refs (mutate ref between renders, observe fresh values), handleEdgeReconnect duplicate detection picks up new edges added to ref after init. reconnectingRef exposed in return, starts false, identity stable. Phase-2 refactors: extracted triplicated `haptic = (ms=50) => navigator?.vibrate?.(ms)` from useMapEdgeActions/useMapNodeActions/KnowledgeMapView into mindmap/hapticHelper.ts (single source of truth, ~10 LOC). Tightened useMapNodeActions interface: setConnectSource and setAnnotationNode now Dispatch<SetStateAction<MapNode|null>> (was loose (node)=>void) — interface now matches the 78-81 callsite usage of `prev => prev?.id === deletedId ? null : prev` updater functions. Updated knowledgeMapView.contract.test.ts to assert haptic-import-from-helper rather than haptic-at-module-scope. smoke.test.ts directory completeness now whitelists hapticHelper.ts (tested transitively via the 3 consumers). Phase-3 security: clean (no findings — escHtml escapes the 5 G6 tooltip user-content paths, no dangerouslySetInnerHTML/eval/document.write in scope). 500-node perf test: PASS (35 tests, 296ms). Result: 74 files / 3619 tests, ALL PASSING.

useMapNodeActions coverage 10→60 (+50): executeDeleteNode setter-updater contract pinned (cycle-44 interface change consequence) — setSelectedNode/setContextMenu/setConnectSource/setAnnotationNode all called as updater functions, not direct values; updater nulls out only when prev.id matches deleted id; preserves unrelated nodes; handles null prev; setContextMenu uses different shape {node,position} so accesses prev?.node.id; all 4 setters NOT called on error path (cleanup only on success). pushAction payload includes label+definition+topic_id, definition can be undefined; pushAction NOT called on error or unmounted; refetch NOT called on error or unmounted. setHistoryEntries called with updater; APPENDS not prepends; entry type='delete-node' badge='Nodo' description contains label; NOT called on error. deletingNodeRef reentrancy guard exposed in return, starts false, identity stable, reset after success+after API error, blocks concurrent calls (deferred-resolver test), early-return paths (null confirmDeleteNode, empty topicId) do NOT touch the ref. mountedRef bail: pushAction/setHistoryEntries/refetch/setConfirmDeleteNode/4-cleanup-setters all NOT called when unmounted before success; toast.error NOT called when unmounted in catch; deletingNodeRef STILL reset to false in finally even when unmounted. deleteCustomNode argument identity: called with exactly confirmDeleteNode.id (string), no extra args. handleNodeCreated: handles payload without definition (label-only), history APPENDS, entry type='create-node' badge='Nodo' description contains label, nodeId string identity preserved through pushAction, topic_id propagated from effectiveTopicId, haptic 50ms vibrate (defineProperty pattern). handleDeleteCustomNode returns Promise (async shape), passes FULL node to setConfirmDeleteNode (not just id), does NOT call deleteCustomNode API (only opens dialog), isUserCreated=undefined treated as falsy, does NOT vibrate. Stable references: handleDeleteCustomNode/executeDeleteNode/handleNodeCreated stable with same deps; executeDeleteNode reference changes on effectiveTopicId or confirmDeleteNode change; handleNodeCreated changes on effectiveTopicId; handleDeleteCustomNode does NOT depend on effectiveTopicId. Phase-2 refactors: extracted wrapAiCall(failurePrefix, fn) helper in src/app/services/mindmapAiApi.ts to dedupe 3× try/catch boilerplate across analyzeKnowledgeGraph/suggestStudentConnections/getStudentWeakPoints (~24 LOC saved, error-cause preservation kept identical). Removed stale 'handleConnectNodes' line from useMapEdgeActions docstring (callback was never returned by the hook). Phase-3 security: clean. NON-SECURITY BUG fixed (caught by security scanner cross-reference): AiTutorPanel.tsx:146 ImprovedCheckmark required {name,label} but call site at :669 only passed name — made label optional with conditional render `{label && ...}` — was a TypeScript shape mismatch that would have rendered an empty `{undefined}` slot in the 'Progreso detectado' panel. 500-node perf test: PASS (35 tests, 305ms). Result: 74 files / 3669 tests, ALL PASSING.

useGraphHighlighting coverage 39→102 (+63). Effect gating (9 tests): ready=false / layoutInProgressRef.current=true / graphRef.current=null all suppress updateNodeData; same Set ref + same epoch on rerender → no-op; epoch increment forces re-apply with same Set ref; new Set ref triggers re-apply; both undefined or both empty + epoch unchanged → no-op; transition non-empty→empty highlight set re-applies (clears styling). Highlighted node styles (11 tests): shadowBlur=10, lineWidth=3, opacity=1, shadowColor=mastery-stroke, labelFill=primary; dimmed (other) nodes: opacity=0.35, labelFill=tertiary, shadowBlur=0, shadowColor='transparent', no lineWidth; needsReview is falsy (not literal false) for highlighted-only — exact behavior captured. Review-only node styles (7 tests): shadowBlur=8, lineWidth=2.5, shadowColor=#f97316, labelFill=#c2410c, label prefix '⚠ ', needsReview=true, opacity=1. Highlight + review overlap precedence (6 tests): highlight wins lineWidth=3, shadowBlur=10; review wins shadowColor=#f97316, labelFill=#c2410c, label prefix; needsReview=true regardless. Edge dimming (5 tests): both endpoints in highlight → opacity=1; one missing → 0.2; neither → 0.2; review-only mode → no edge dimming; edges with invisible endpoints SKIPPED entirely (no updateEdgeData call). Visibility filter (2 tests): nodes not in graph.getNodeData() are skipped; getNodeData throws → fallback updates ALL dataNodesRef nodes. batchDraw interaction (2 tests): called once per highlight effect run; not on early-return. Selected node effect (12 tests): null selection on mount → no update; mount with selection → 1 update with shadowBlur=12; switching: 1 updateNodeData call with 2 entries (clear prev + apply new); deselecting → 1 entry clearing prev shadow; same selectedNodeId rerender → no-op; absent from nodeById → no update; undefined treated as null; null graphRef doesn't crash; ready=false suppresses. applyMultiSelectionState corner cases (7 tests): outer try/catch swallows setElementState throws; inner catch falls back to ['multiSelected'] when getElementState throws; re-adding existing 'multiSelected' doesn't duplicate; unchanged ids in both prev and curr cause zero setElementState calls (efficient diff); callback identity stable across rerenders when batchDraw ref stable; identity changes when batchDraw ref changes; prevMultiRef instance stable. Label truncation (2 tests): truncateLabel applied BEFORE '⚠ ' prefix; non-review nodes don't get prefix. Test mock fix: added devWarn:vi.fn() to ../graphHelpers mock since source calls devWarn in getNodeData catch path. Phase-2 audit findings re-evaluated: handleUpdateStickyNote/handleDeleteStickyNote in useMapStickyNotes are dead in production (only KnowledgeMapView consumes stickyNotes/setStickyNotes/handleAddStickyNote, StickyNotesLayer mutates via raw setter) but heavily test-pinned (~7 test cases) — same precedent as cycle-45's handleDeleteCustomNode: treat as live API contract, defer removal. Phase-3 security: clean — verified prototype pollution NOT exploitable on highlightNodeIds/reviewNodeIds Sets (Set.prototype.has uses internal-slot lookup, no [[Get]] against prototype chain; no plain object keyed by attacker-controlled IDs). Forward-looking calibration item logged: useGraphInit.ts:610-616/641-644 tooltip style attrs interpolate hardcoded constants — if a future commit ever inserts user data into a style='...' literal, escHtml does not neutralize CSS injection (expression(), url(javascript:), attribute-breakout). 500-node perf test: PASS (35 tests, 292ms). Result: 74 files / 3732 tests, ALL PASSING.

useFocusTrap coverage 7→46 (+39, ~6.6× coverage). 12 behavior groups pinned: ref shape (3 — defined, current=null initially, stable identity); initial focus (5 — focuses first focusable on activation, rAF must fire (no focus before timer flush), rAF cancelled if deactivated mid-flight, null ref no-throw, single-element activation); Tab forward wrap (2 — last→first preventDefaults, middle Tab is no-op no preventDefault); Shift+Tab backward wrap (2 — first→last preventDefaults, middle Shift+Tab no-op); single focusable element (2 — Tab and Shift+Tab both preventDefault and self-refocus, first===last collapse); RECAPTURE on focus escape (2 — both Tab and Shift+Tab recapture to FIRST when activeElement is outside container, asymmetry pinned as design choice not bug); zero focusable elements (2 — Tab and Shift+Tab preventDefault, fallback path executes); non-Tab keys ignored (7 parameterized — Enter, Escape, Space, ArrowDown, ArrowUp, 'a', F1 all leave defaultPrevented=false and activeElement unchanged); active gating (4 — active=false leaves outside focus alone, no Tab response, toggling true→false detaches listener, reactivation captures fresh previouslyFocused per cycle); focus restoration (4 — restored on deactivate, restored on unmount-while-active, survives previouslyFocused removed from DOM no throw, document.body baseline no-prior-focus no throw); focusable selector (10 — a[href] included but bare <a> excluded, disabled button/input/textarea/select all skipped, tabindex=0 and positive included, tabindex=-1 excluded, contenteditable NOT included pinned current behavior, Tab cycle skips disabled correctly); listener placement (3 — listener on container only NOT document, sibling keydown does NOT fire, bubbling from inner children DOES reach handler, cleanup removes listener). Phase-2 BIG FINDING — overturned cycle-48 carry-forward: scout did concrete LOC analysis on the pointerCancelRef bridge fold and found NET +6 LOC delta (saves ~4 across both hosts but adds ~10-12 to helper) PLUS a 50+ LOC closure-lift refactor to extract handlePointerCancel from its main interaction effect (which closes over graph, container, overlayCanvasRef, isDraggingRef, safeReleasePointerCapture + host-specific G6 calls). Bridge is structural, not accidental. Documented in useEscapeCancel.ts header so future cycles don't re-evaluate the same dead-end. Phase-3 security: clean (6th consecutive). useFocusTrap audit confirmed safe — listener on container NOT document (no cross-React-boundary leakage), FOCUSABLE_SELECTOR is module-level const (no template interpolation, no CSS-selector injection vector), document.contains guard before previouslyFocused.focus() avoids focusing detached node, requestAnimationFrame paired with cancelAnimationFrame on cleanup, keydown listener removed with matching args. 500-node perf: 35/35 in 341ms. Result: 76 files / 3953 tests, ALL PASSING (+39 over cycle 48). NEXT-CYCLE STARTING POINT: Phase-2 also confirmed useDragActivation extraction is BELOW 15-LOC threshold (7 LOC truly common per site after stripping host tails); KnowledgeMapView 986 LOC and KnowledgeGraph 699 LOC are tracked-debt but already heavily decomposed.

REFACTOR cycle (took the deferred cycle-47 extractions). Created useOverlayCanvas.ts (98 LOC) and useEscapeCancel.ts (54 LOC) extracting two HIGH-priority sister-hook duplications (~50 + ~16 LOC) from useDragConnect/useEdgeReconnect. useOverlayCanvas: canvas creation + DPR resize + ResizeObserver + parentNode-guarded cleanup; z-index supplied by caller (5 in useEdgeReconnect, 6 in useDragConnect); enabled=false short-circuits; containerRef.current=null at mount short-circuits. useEscapeCancel: document keydown listener with {capture:true}; preventDefault+stopPropagation BEFORE onCancel; gated by isActive() so unrelated Escape presses propagate when no drag in flight. Test migration: 17 useDragConnect tests + 5 useEdgeReconnect tests rewritten in place to assert delegation (`expect(source).toContain('useOverlayCanvas')`, `expect(source).toContain('useEscapeCancel')`, NEGATIVE assertions like `not.toContain('new ResizeObserver')` — STRONGER than removed-pattern checks). 0 tests deleted. New test files: useOverlayCanvas.test.ts (33 tests) and useEscapeCancel.test.ts (19 tests) covering exports/types, canvas creation, DPR math (replicated 5x), cleanup symmetry, manual resize callback, listener registration, Escape semantics, dep array stability, replicated escape gate (4x). LOC: useDragConnect 567→559 (-8), useEdgeReconnect 579→573 (-6); +152 LOC of new helpers; net ~50 LOC of duplication eliminated. Behavior subtleties preserved: (1) Two effects in useDragConnect now where there was one — canvas via helper, button via host effect; cleanup order reverse-declaration so canvas tears down after button (was the same order before); (2) pointerCancelRef bridge — host populates pointerCancelRef.current=handlePointerCancel at effect mount, nulls on cleanup; helper checks dragStateRef.current!==null before invoking, so brief refnull window during cleanup is safe (matches pre-extraction behavior — listener was unbound during same window); (3) ctxRef exposed but unused by callers — both hosts still call overlay.getContext('2d') in draw loops (harmless, flagged for future cleanup); (4) useEdgeReconnect rAF cancellation moved into auxiliary cleanup effect gated on enabled&&ready; (5) cleanup order tear-down symmetric across both hosts. NEW DEBT introduced (MEDIO): pointerCancelRef bridge boilerplate (~15 LOC across two hosts) is a side-effect of moving handlePointerCancel out of useEscapeCancel's reach. Net dedup gain real but smaller than headline numbers (66 dup-LOC removed - 15 bridge-LOC added = ~51 effective). Phase-2 verification: 5/5 PASS — no document.createElement('canvas') or document.addEventListener('keydown') remain in either host (only div for quick-connect button); helpers single-export single-purpose typed clean JSDoc'd; no commented-out code; quick-connect button setup intact. Phase-3 security: clean — 5th consecutive. zIndex is `number` typed param so cssText injection impossible; document {capture:true} listener is gated, no cross-boundary leakage; pointerCancelRef TOCTOU explicitly verified single-threaded-safe in both unmount orderings. 500-node perf: 35/35 in 303ms. Result: 76 files (was 74, +2 helper test files) / 3914 tests (+54 from new helper tests), ALL PASSING. NEXT-CYCLE RECOMMENDATION (Phase-2 carry-forward): fold pointerCancelRef bridge into useEscapeCancel's API to eliminate the small new debt this cycle introduced; OR extract useDragActivation for the overlay-flip + pointer-capture + isDraggingRef pattern (~12 LOC dedup, moderate complexity).

useDragConnect RE-CYCLE: 67→195 (+128). Cycle 21 was NOT saturating — it pinned the pointer state machine in tight focus (~30% of file surface) but left ~70% unpinned. This cycle covered the rest. Hover detection (7): HOVER_THROTTLE_MS time-gate, lazy position-cache recompute, node.size/2+10 hit radius (deliberately tighter than drag's PORT_HIT_RADIUS=24), identity-only redraw transitions, first-hit break (no overlap resolution), drag-skips-hover early return, lastPointerTypeRef mutation on every move. Hover-cache invalidation (3): afterviewportchange flips cache flag, effect re-binds on graphVersion swap, try/catch around graph.off. Quick-connect button positioning (9): exact upper-right offset (x+size/2+2, y-size/2-2), per-pointer-type sizing, show/hide on hover transition, hide on activation, mouse-default initial size, z-index layering (6 above 5), pointer-events:auto for click delegation, GRAPH_COLORS wiring. Quick-connect click handler (7): mousedown (not click), removeEventListener symmetry, preventDefault+stopPropagation, hoveredNodeRef.current (not e.target), no-op when callback missing, ref-pattern for onQuickAdd AND onDragConnect. Drag activation side effects (8): setPointerCapture, capturedPointerId recorded, initial -1 sentinel, overlay pointerEvents='auto', cursor='grabbing', hoveredNodeRef cleared, activated=true post-threshold, isDraggingRef.current=true. Pointer-up branches (7): non-activated short-circuit (no DOM), failure clears overlay (clearRect ≥2 times), failure does NOT populate successAnimRef, symmetric tear-down, overlay restored, rAF cancelled before re-scheduling, callback fires BEFORE optimistic edge-set insertion. Pointer-cancel branches (4): DOM cleanup gated on ds.activated, state-reset outside the gate, no-op when no active drag, rAF-before-clearRect order. Synthetic Escape (4): captured pointerId forwarded, preventDefault+stopPropagation before dispatch, gated on dragStateRef.current, listener on document (not container). DPR canvas resize (5): pixel buffer = rect × DPR rounded, CSS = raw rect, ||1 fallback, ResizeObserver.observe + disconnect. Draw loop continuation (7): three-way OR (needsNext||activated||hovered), rAF (not setTimeout), early-returns on missing overlay/ctx/container, pointer-aware portRadius, locale-aware connectToLabel. Success animation auto-clear (5): elapsed/SUCCESS_ANIM_DURATION>=1 gate, performance.now() measurement, ref nulled, tgtX/tgtY from snap (not cursor), srcX/srcY from source center. Effect dep arrays (6) all 4 useEffect + draw + isDragging useCallback deps individually. Mount-time guards (6): !enabled||!ready early-out, container/graph null checks, position:relative set, append to container (not body). Cleanup discipline (10): ref-null counts (≥3 dragStateRef=null, ≥2 hoveredNodeRef=null, ≥3 cancelAnimationFrame, ≥3 isDraggingRef=false), parentNode guards on remove. Drag-state seeding (8): startX/Y, dragX/Y, activated:false, snapNodeId:null, cachedPositions snapshot, performance.now() startTime, source geometry seeding, single-source return after seed. Snap precedence (1): self-loop check BEFORE duplicate (otherwise wrong reason string). Re-entry guards (2): dragStateRef!==null and sister isDraggingRef.current rejection. Locale fallback (3): Spanish (I18N_GRAPH.es) fallback, 4-string t object, btn.title from i18n. Overlap-aware drag rejection (3): first pass scans ALL nodes for inner-60% before second pass picks port; for-of returns on first port hit; both pointerdown and hover use getNodeScreenPositions(graph, true). Replicated behavioral pins (18): DPR math at 1×/2×/3×/0/1.5×; drag-start hit-zone classification (skip-inner/drag-port/outside) at 6 boundaries; hover hit-test (replicated) at 5 cases. Phase-2 deferred: scout flagged HIGH overlay-canvas lifecycle dup (~50 LOC) between useDragConnect/useEdgeReconnect (extract useOverlayCanvas) and HIGH escape-key handler dup (~16 LOC) — deferred to next cycle because the new tests already pin overlay-creation behavior on the existing structure and the new shared hook would itself be 25-30 LOC of new code. Phase-3 security: 4th consecutive clean audit. Coordinate-attack analysis: NaN/Infinity from synthetic events would only fail < comparisons (always false), so worst-case is degraded UX, not privilege escalation. DOM listener pairing symmetric across all add/remove sites in both hooks. Pointer-capture lifecycle protected by safeReleasePointerCapture. 500-node perf test: PASS (35 tests, 323ms). Result: 74 files / 3860 tests, ALL PASSING. NOTABLE: largest single-cycle test addition (+128) since the early cycles.

cumulative.milestone: PERFECT — 76/76 files pass, 3953/3953 assertions pass; cycle #49 boosted useFocusTrap (7→46, ~6.6× coverage) on a never-cycled accessibility-critical hook AND closed a carry-forward refactor proposal with hard data — scout proved the pointerCancelRef bridge fold would NET +6 LOC (not the assumed -15) and require 50+ LOC of closure-lifting. Documentation comment added to useEscapeCancel.ts so the loop doesn't reconsider the dead-end. Loop self-correcting: cycle 48 proposed → cycle 49 evaluated → documented as intentional.
